reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: All 3 gates passed. Gate 1: auctionCallback exists at AuctionBidder.sol:97 and has a live non-test caller at BaseAuction.sol:449; it also implements IAuctionCallback (public interface). Gate 2: the attack path flows through an AUCTION_BIDDER_ROLE external solver via AuctionBidder.bid() — trust_assumptions maps this to 'bidders: UNTRUSTED' (in_scope_for_bug_bounty: true). Gate 3: src/AuctionBidder.sol is explicitly listed in scope. An attacker holding AUCTION_BIDDER_ROLE can call AuctionBidder.bid() with a solution containing Call({target: heldToken, data: transfer(attacker, balance)}); the auction transfers assetIn to AuctionBidder then invokes auctionCallback passing the attacker-controlled data verbatim, and _multiCall executes the transfer with no target or calldata restrictions, draining any token held by the AuctionBidder contract before the auction's safeTransferFrom for assetOut runs. Severity is Critical per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE ('Direct theft or permanent freezing of funds'); no client_diversity cap applies.
